commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0c0dba024275f41eef629f8db8415fa21e095ac1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a guarded `reader.releaseLock()` call in the Ollama raw stream cleanup path before the existing SSRF guard release.

PR surface: Source +3. Total +3 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defect: CI typecheck logs and source inspection reproduce the out-of-scope `reader` reference. No high-confidence live reproduction of the original Ollama resource leak has been supplied.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The branch needs a contributor or maintainer code fix plus contributor-owned real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not queue repair while the external proof gate remains unsatisfied.

target.title: fix(ollama): release stream reader lock to prevent resource leak
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts:1463` acquires a `ReadableStream` reader via `response.body?.getReader()` but does not call `reader.releaseLock()` in the finally block. If the stream processing throws, the reader lock is leaked — the underlying stream remains locked and cannot be reused or garbage-collected.

## Changes

- `extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts`: add `reader.releaseLock()` (wrapped in try/catch) before `await release()` in the finally block

This matches the defensive lock-release pattern used elsewhere in the codebase where stream readers are acquired and must be released regardless of success or failure.

Label: bugfix | merge-risk: 🟢 minimal | AI-assisted

## Evidence

### Code path analysis

The affected function `createRawOllamaStreamFn`:
1. Acquires `reader = response.body?.getReader()` 
2. Enters a `while (true)` loop reading chunks
3. Has a `finally` block that calls `await release()` but did NOT call `reader.releaseLock()`

If any iteration of the read loop throws (e.g., parse error, abort), the finally block runs `release()` but the reader lock remains held. Adding `reader.releaseLock()` in the same defensive try/catch pattern ensures the lock is released.

### Risk assessment

The change adds 3 lines (`try { reader.releaseLock(); } catch {}`) inside an existing finally block. The try/catch wrap ensures that even if `releaseLock()` itself throws (e.g., reader already released), the `await release()` path still executes.
target.author: lsr911

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
